flows.name: Pull request redteam review
flows.description: A contributor opens a PR and CodeDecay reports what could break, whether tests prove the behavior, and what the agent should fix before merge.
checks: Run codedecay redteam against the PR diff
Check weak or missing test proof
Review edge cases and agent fix tasks
Keep deterministic evidence separate from AI suggestions

flows.name: CLI release smoke
flows.description: The published package and built CLI must work like a new user would install and run them.
checks: Run published-package or tarball demo before release

flows.name: GitHub Action dogfood
flows.description: The local composite action must run on real PRs, write a useful summary, and preserve fail-on behavior.
checks: Confirm CodeDecay dogfood check runs on the PR

flows.name: PR safety efficacy benchmark
flows.description: Seeded regressions must prove CodeDecay catches weak tests, missing test proof, impacted areas, edge cases, and actionable fix tasks.
checks: Confirm normal tests pass while behavior probes catch seeded regressions
Confirm edge cases and fix tasks are actionable

commands.name: Full validation
commands.command: pnpm install && pnpm run lint && pnpm typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm build
commands.description: Baseline validation required before PR merge.

commands.name: Package dry-run
commands.description: Verifies published package contents and npm metadata.

invariants.name: No hidden cloud or model call
invariants.description: The OSS CLI must remain useful without telemetry, API keys, hosted services, required LLM calls, or CodeDecayCloud.

invariants.name: Commands are explicit
invariants.description: CodeDecay must not run project commands unless they are configured and safety.allowCommands is true.

invariants.name: Tool evidence is separate from AI suggestions
invariants.description: Reports must not present agent/model suggestions as verified evidence unless backed by deterministic checks or command output.

invariants.name: Output must be actionable
invariants.description: Redteam reports and agent bundles should say what behavior to verify, which test proof is weak or missing, and what task a coding agent should perform.

invariants.name: Package remains installable from npm
invariants.description: The published package must include README, LICENSE, package.json, dist/index.js, and dist/index.d.ts without workspace-only dependency leaks.

architecture.title: CLI is the published surface
architecture.note: The public npm package is @submuxhq/codedecay and the binary is codedecay. Internal workspace packages are implementation details.

architecture.title: Local-first redteam workflow
architecture.note: codedecay redteam is deterministic and report-only by default. Command execution and LLM/provider use require explicit user configuration.

architecture.title: Agent-owned workflow
architecture.note: CodeDecay should produce evidence and task bundles that a user's own Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Pi, desktop app, or MCP client can use.

architecture.title: Evals protect the product promise
architecture.note: The PR safety eval is the first regression harness for whether CodeDecay catches AI-overlooked PR risks, not just whether unit tests pass.

regressions.title: Package directory with test in name misclassified as test file
regressions.description: packages/test-audit/src/index.ts was once treated as a test file because path matching used the whole path instead of segment/basename-aware test detection.
regressions.check: Regression test: packages/test-audit/src/index.ts is source, while test/ and __tests__/ paths remain tests.

regressions.title: Redteam output had non-actionable edge cases
regressions.description: Edge-case output previously included bare file paths and repeated generic fix-task titles, which made agent handoff less useful.
regressions.check: Run pnpm eval:pr-safety and confirm edge cases and fix tasks are actionable.

regressions.title: Published npm package leaked workspace protocol
regressions.description: Published package installation failed when workspace protocol dependencies leaked into the npm package.
regressions.check: Install @submuxhq/codedecay in a fresh project and run codedecay --help.

regressions.title: NPM tarball missed release hygiene files
regressions.description: The package previously needed fixes to include README and LICENSE in the npm tarball.
regressions.check: Run pnpm --filter @submuxhq/codedecay pack --dry-run and inspect contents.
